article_title: Tesla Faces March 9 NHTSA Deadline to Hand Over FSD Crash Data as Austin Robotaxi Fleet Crashes at Four Times the Human Rate

content_preview.title: Tesla Faces March 9 NHTSA Deadline to Hand Over FSD Crash Data as Austin Robotaxi Fleet Crashes at Four Times the Human Rate
content_preview.summary: NHTSA gave Tesla a final March 9 deadline to deliver crash video and telemetry from its FSD investigation, while data shows Austin robotaxis crash four times more often than human drivers.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Tesla has until March 9, 2026 to deliver critical crash video, event data recorder files, and CAN bus telemetry to the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration as part of a widening federal investigation into its Full Self-Driving software. The deadline, already extended twice from its original January 19 date, arrives as newly compiled data shows Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet is experiencing collisions at roughly four times the rate of human drivers by the company's own metri...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is well-structured, factually dense, and appropriately neutral in tone. The 835-word Analysis-category piece effectively contextualizes the NHTSA investigation deadline against comparative crash rate data. The rewrite maintains strong technical precision throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: Four sources are cited. Three are from outlets on the allowlist (Electrek, Gizmodo, Mercury News). The fourth — safety21.cmu.edu (Carnegie Mellon Safety21 center) — remains off the allowlist but is a legitimate academic source whose claims are corroborated by multiple mainstream outlets. This is a carry-over from Round 1 and does not block approval.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Both required corrections have been independently verified and confirmed present in this submission. The 90% serious-injury reduction figure now matches published Waymo safety research. The ten-city count correctly reflects Waymo's February 24, 2026 expansion. No new factual issues were introduced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and measured throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no editorializing. Comparative crash statistics are presented with appropriate methodological context (Tesla's own Vehicle Safety Report vs. NHTSA police-reported standards).
editor_notes.originality: The article synthesizes a timely regulatory deadline with independently reported crash rate analysis, providing genuine editorial value beyond simple aggregation.
editor_notes.changes_verified: Waymo injury reduction corrected to 90% (was 80%), Waymo city count corrected to ten (was six). Both corrections are present and properly attributed to sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: All required changes from Round 1 have been correctly applied. The article meets publication standards. Approved for publication.
